sourceMovie.title: Bridesmaids
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Bridesmaids (2011) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pitch Perfect
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bridesmaids clicked for you, Pitch Perfect (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bridesmaids clicked for you, Poor Things (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bridesmaids?
faq.answer: Try Pitch Perfect, Poor Things, Wedding Crashers—each shares DNA with Bridesmaids while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bridesmaids?
faq.answer: Bridesmaids (2011) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bridesmaids worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
